eldora.note: 5:39 AM update – Good morning, skiers and riders! Welcome to the Eldora snow report for Wednesday, March 25th, 2026. Happy Wednesday, everyone! Come on up and enjoy some incredible spring skiing conditions! The snow will be soft and there will be hardly any lift lines. Weekdays are the best days to ski and ride Eldora, and you'll realize why after a day of skiing and riding here. Also, it's a Play Forever Wednesday with $99 lift tickets, and a portion of sales go to supporting local nonprofits . NWS Weather Snapshot: Sunny, with a high near 66. Breezy, with a west wind 18 to 21 mph, with gusts as high as 34 mph. 2026-27 season passes are now on sale . Lock in your lowest price now, before prices increase on April 30. Uphill and Nordic Skiing... The Nordic Center is now closed for the season. Check Nordic Pulse for the latest update. The final day of Uphill Access is Friday, March 27. Vist eldora.com/uphill for more info. Reminder: Spring Hours are now in effect through Closing Day. Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week Stay in the loop: This page, eldora.com/conditions , is always your go-to for the latest updates, but you can also sign up for text alerts so you never miss a beat. Text one of the following keywords to (866) 965-6224: ELDORA for general alerts, UPHILL for uphill-specific updates, or NORDIC for Nordic updates. Message and data rates may apply. Thanks for checking the Eldora snow report. We'll see you on the hill! -- GENERAL INFORMATION Lift Hours: 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, seven days a week. Lodge Hours: 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM, seven days a week. Single-Occupancy Vehicle (SOV) Fees: Vehicles with two or more occupants may park for free seven days a week. Vehicles with one occupant will be charged $10 on weekends, during certain holiday periods, and on weekdays with 10 or more inches of new snow. Credit/debit is accepted; cash is not. High-Occupancy Vehicle (HOV) Parking: Per Boulder County requirements, the Elk Lot (nearest to Alpenglow chair) is reserved seven days a week for vehicles with three or more occupants on a first-come, first-served basis. Learn more here . EV Parking: Eldora provides 30 free ChargePoint EV charging stations. These premium parking spots are available on a first-come, first-served basis and are subject to the SOV policy above. To park in the EV lot, you MUST have an EV and be actively charging. If you are finished charging, please move your car to another non-EV space so others can use the chargers. Easy, Convenient RTD Bus Service from Boulder: RTD’s NB route brings you from Downtown Boulder Station straight to the Alpenglow chairlift in about 40 minutes; riders 19 and under ride RTD for FREE through RTD's Zero Fare for Youth program. For schedule and fare info, visit rtd-denver.com

killington.note: Good evening, Beast family, Clear and sunny skies met soft spring snow today and we had a blast shredding around. There’s more to be psyched about as this weekend looks to bring more sunshine. A little bit of everything rolls through overnight with spotty snow showers and a touch of mix, while winds ramp up and keep things moving. Thursday brings snow showers before 9:00 a.m., then scattered rain showers and strong winds. Temperatures will be pushing to 49 at the base and 43 at the top with just a trace of new snow at elevation. Surface conditions are holding some hardpack still, but trails are softening up, especially where the sun shines strong like over at Bear. Skyeburst and Dream Maker had beautifully soft snow that made for a lot of fun. We could see some precipitation, but temperatures should stay well above freezing keeping surfaces on the softer trend. Head over to Bear and to Needles Eye for that more carvable snow. Our grooming team is adding Upper Wildfire to the list of trails that will be resurfaced by the time first chair rolls around. East Fall and Highline will be groomed and were holding decent surfaces. Head over to Caper or Bear Claw for a more chill ride that will also be smoothed over. For the full list of groomed options, head to our Lifts & Trails Report . Our snowmaking team took advantage of the cold stretch last week, firing on Snowshed Slope, Upper Skyelark, Lower Bittersweet, and Highlander. Stay tuned for what's to come as they’ll be getting the guns going again soon, and this is the latest they've ever had them running! If you haven’t yet, check out our latest Insider Blog to see how our recent snowmaking investments have been paying off. Our key lifts are once again scheduled to open again at 9:00 a.m., with the North Ridge Quad following suit at 9:30 a.m. and our South Ridge Quad coming online at 10:00 a.m. In our kparks, the Bear parks (Dream Maker & The Stash) are back on the menu for some sweet spring skiing & riding. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 44 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe. Our Killington Parks Crew recently did a feature refresh in Timberline, and our halfpipe was re-cut again this past evening. Make sure you check out what the team has been cooking up! Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are also dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on all of our features. Last thing: if you can't get enough of spring at The Beast, grab a 2026 Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding through the final day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek, and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade anytime for just $199. Let’s get out there. - Evan Operating Day: 135 Lifts Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends. A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1! The Tubing Park presented by bubly is closed for the season. Uphill Travel Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific uphill route information, including a map of each route: Ramshead base to top of Ramshead Express Quad - Open Ramshead Express Quad to top of Snowdon Mountain - Open Snowshed base to the top of Snowshed Slope - Open Base of Sunrise Village Triple to top of Bear Mountain - Open Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect - CLOSED Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open Pico Summit Glade Route - CLOSED Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here . All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours. MOUNTAIN SERVICES Parking & Shuttles Parking is available at the K-1, Snowshed, Ramshead, Bear, Skyeship and Vale parking lots. Please respect signage and allow our parking staff to assist you on your trip to the slopes. Shuttle service is available from 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. midweek and 6:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends and holidays. Winter car camping and overnight parking is only available in the lot across from Skyeship Lodge. See details here . Lodge Services All lodges are open daily from 8:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekdays and 7:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. on weekends/peak dates*. K-1 Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. daily. Brew Bar (2nd floor): Open serving handcrafted coffees at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 7:30 a.m. on weekends, with cocktails and beers served starting at 9:00 a.m. K-1 Pub (3rd floor): Open at 10:00 a.m. daily. Rentals & Killington Sports at K-1: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Guest Services: 1st floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 1:00 p.m. on weekdays and until 4:30 p.m. on weekends/peak dates. 2nd floor - Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Superstar Umbrella Bar: Open at 11:00 a.m. from Friday - Sunday offering beer, wine, liquor, and specialty hot cocktails. Snowshed Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served from 10:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. daily. Long Trail Pub (3rd floor): Open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., offering our Lunch Dining Menu until 5:30 p.m. and lite fare until 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Umbrella Bar: Open on Saturdays and Sundays serving beers, cocktails, and light fare. Rentals, Killington Sports at Snowshed, & Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. On Fridays, rentals & demos for the weekend can be picked up ahead of time between 2:00 p.m. and 6:00 p.m. Snowshed Demos & Tuning: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 30 minutes after lifts. Ski & snowboard demo packages available. Please note that binding mounts and adjustments are not offered at this time. Click here for more information. Private & Adult Group Lessons: Booked online in advance here . Ramshead Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on Fridays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Served daily with grab & go options & soups. The pizza station is open Thursday - Monday, and the full grill menu is available Friday - Sunday. Guest Services: Open 30 minutes before lifts until 12:00 p.m. on Monday – Thursday and until 4:30 p.m. on Friday - Sunday at the ticket windows outside of the lodge. Rentals: Open for youth lesson participants and families only. Youth Lessons: Youth Group Lessons and our Ministars program meet at Ramshead. Child Care Center: Open weekdays from 8:30 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. and weekends from 8:00 a.m. - 3:45 p.m. Bear Mountain Lodge Breakfast: Served starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and 7:30 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Lunch: Open daily with grab & go items and soups available. Full grill menu available Friday - Sunday. Bear Bar: Open from 10:00 a.m. until 4:30 p.m. on weekdays and until 5:00 p.m. on weekends. Guest Services: Available Friday - Monday at ticket windows outside the lodge. Open 30 minutes before lifts open. Kiosk lift ticket pickup is available daily. Skyeship Lodge Food & Bar Service: Available daily from The Rollin’ Rooster. Guest Services: Open daily inside the lodge. Closing Date: March 29th On-Mountain Dining Options Peak Lodge – Open daily from 9:30 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. Grill open daily from 10:30 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. Peak Lodge Bar offers its own menu daily with QR code ordering. Jerk Jamaican Mountain Grill – Ski-on, ski- off. Open Thursday-Monday from 11:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. weather permitting. Ledgewood Yurt – closed for the season. Mountain services are subject to change. Please check here daily before making your lunch and lodge choices. Winter Lift Tickets and rentals must be purchased online at this link . Check to see if your pass is good to go using our Online Pass Checker . Find Season Pass FAQs by clicking here . Get your Unlimited Winter Season Pass today! Buy now . Have a ticketing question? Our text number ( 802-523-2202 ) is the best way to get it answered quickly, but please note that we cannot process transactions via text. Have a season pass question? Call 1-800-621-MTNS or email seasonpass@killington.com Please visit Killington.com/cancellation for more details on our cancellation policy. Killington Sports at Route 4 is open Sunday - Thursday from 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m., Friday and Saturday from 10:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m., and 10:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. on holidays. Killington is a cashless resort. We accept all major credit cards as well as Apple, Google, and Samsung Pay. *No remaining Peak Dates during winter 2025/26. We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

copper mountain.note: Good morning, faithful snow report readers! Here's your sun report for Wednesday, 3/25. We're looking at another scorcher today, with highs at mid-mountain expected to approach 60F in the afternoon hours. Winds will be light out of the west at 5-10mph with the occasional gust into the 30s. This trend continues into tomorrow before a chance a slightly colder temps and some precip overnight into Friday morning. If you haven't done so already, get that fresh wax applied to your gear to best combat the slush fest going on out there. Conditions have been evolving minute to minute during this heat wave. Ease into things and remember that your favorite run might ski very differently from one lap to the next. Don't forget to reapply that sunscreen, take in lots of water and as always, make the most of it...we're still skiing!

snowbird.note: Resort Update: 8 am - 2 pm spring lift operations are expected to remain in place through Sunday, March 29. Hours of operation will be reassessed for the following week. Snow Report: Gooooood morning Snowbird! It’s Owen, with your snow report for Wednesday. Today is going to be a hot one. We are going to have a high of 68° today and sunny skies all day. It’s been getting real surfy out there, but I’m still having a great time. Wiggles have started to come out of hibernation for the spring which have been pretty fun - if you don't do at least one wiggle lap, did you really go spring skiing? (Also I'm gonna need y'all to smooth those things out a bit, they're still a bit rowdy and I feel like I'm going to eat it on every turn). As you’ve just read above, we will be transitioning to spring hours now as late afternoon has just been getting a bit too warm. Additionally, scenic tram hours will be shifting to 8 am - 1 pm and ticket offices will be open 8 am - 1:30 pm. Hey you! Yeah you! Do you like skiing or riding at Snowbird? Do you like saving money? Well great news, Snowbird Season Passes for 2026/27 are on sale now! Buy now to get the cheapest price (and maybe a little bonus). If that sounds good to you, click this link . The longer you wait, the more the price increases, so don’t miss out. Here’s one more link , just in case you missed the first one. Trust me, I’d buy early if I were you - it’s worth it. Today is Play Forever Wednesday . $5 of your ticket will be donated to a non-profit of the week that is dedicated to bettering the environment, providing local relief or broadening participation in outdoor activities. This week, we are supporting Women on the Ascent. Their mission is to create an inclusive and empowering outdoor community for women, encouraging them to explore the mountains, build confidence and foster meaningful connections through shared adventures. Click the link to learn more. ALL uphill travel is closed. Don’t be that guy (or gal). Please remember to ski and ride in control, if you don’t, you’re gonna have a bad time. That’s the snow report and if you don’t like it, too bad. I’ll be here all season. Except for Fridays and Saturdays - I’m off those days. #GMTB
